description: Wave 6: Compact civilians, families, station life. Not everyone in space is military. 25 subjects showing the full range of Compact society.
style_prefix: oil painting, semi-realistic painterly character concept art, muted dusty palette, subtle dark edges, soft upper-left directional lighting, worn and functional aesthetic, lived-in sci-fi spacefaring civilization, visible material texture and surface grain, visible brushstrokes, full body visible, front-facing relaxed standing pose, isolated on plain dark grey background
negative: photorealistic, photograph, photo, stock photo, 3d render, smooth CG, CGI, octane render, unreal engine, anime, cartoon, chibi, bright saturated colors, clean pristine new clothing, glamorous, heroic pose, dynamic action pose, multiple characters, busy background, environmental background, landscape, text, watermark, blurry, low quality, extra limbs, deformed, medieval, fantasy, superhero

subjects.prompt: middle-aged human male shopkeeper on a Compact station with a round belly and a merchant smile, wearing a faded blue-grey civilian tunic with a station commerce license badge pinned to the breast, a money belt under the tunic, an apron with pockets for data chits and small change, comfortable station shoes, reading glasses on a cord, he sells ship supplies from a small shop and has opinions about everything

tough human woman in her 40s who runs a bar on a Compact station, wearing a dark grey practical blouse with rolled-up sleeves showing scarred forearms from years of breaking up fights, a bar apron stained with a decade of spilled drinks, anti-slip shoes, a personal defense weapon holstered under the bar counter visible as a bulge at her hip, her earrings are the only personal touch

thin human male school teacher on a Compact station in his 60s with wire-rimmed glasses and chalk dust on his fingers, wearing a faded steel-blue civilian jacket over a white shirt with the collar button undone, comfortable old trousers, scuffed shoes, a data tablet and stylus in his breast pocket, he teaches children whose parents work on ships and he has never been on one himself

stout elderly human woman of chinese descent who sells hot food from a cart on the docking level, wearing layers of warm clothing under a food-stained apron because the docking level is cold, thermal gloves with the fingertips cut off, a headscarf, station-issue flat shoes, a price board written in three languages hanging from her cart, she has been here longer than most of the station staff

pale thin human male bureaucrat in his 40s who has never left the station his entire life, wearing a perfectly maintained steel-blue Compact civil service uniform with a clerks department patch, a laminated ID badge on a retractable reel, a data tablet and two backup data tablets, orthopedic shoes, his uniform is not worn from action but from sitting at the same desk for twenty years

human woman in her 30s with sharp eyes and a press credentials badge, wearing civilian clothes that blend in anywhere — a dark grey jacket, a plain thermal top, dark trousers, quiet shoes, a small recorder device clipped to her collar, a notebook pad in her back pocket even though everything is digital, she dresses to not be noticed which is how she gets stories

gaunt human male in his 30s carrying everything he owns in a single bag, wearing a relief agency thermal blanket draped over whatever clothes he had when his station was evacuated — a torn civilian shirt, mismatched trousers, shoes that do not fit, a temporary ID bracelet on his wrist issued by Compact emergency services, hollow eyes and a posture that has given up on dignity

human woman in her 40s with her hair uncared for and a child-sized jacket draped over her arm that belongs to someone not present, wearing a Compact emergency relief jumpsuit in grey that is one size fits nobody, disposable station shoes, a numbered processing bracelet, she stands with the blank patience of someone waiting in a system that does not see her

human boy about ten years old who has grown up on a Compact station and never seen a planet, wearing a small version of the charcoal station thermal undersuit with a school badge on the shoulder, sneakers that are slightly too big because children outgrow shoes in low gravity, a backpack with textbook data tablets, a toy ship model clipped to the backpack, he is bored and restless like all station children

elderly human woman in her 80s using a walking cane with a magnetic tip for station corridors, wearing a comfortable soft blue-grey cardigan over a thermal undergarment, loose trousers, non-slip slippers, a faded service ribbon pinned to the cardigan from a war that ended forty years ago, a personal medical alert pendant, she moves slowly through the station that has been her home since she retired from the fleet

human woman in her 30s who works the docking level bars, wearing civilian clothes that are slightly more colorful than station standard — a fitted dark red top, dark trousers, low boots, subtle jewelry, her clothes are not provocative just slightly warmer in color than the grey world around her, a personal comm device, she looks tired and practical not glamorous

heavyset human male sanitation worker in his 50s with a resigned expression, wearing a dark grey station maintenance jumpsuit with fluorescent green sanitation department stripes, heavy rubber gloves, anti-contamination boots, a respirator mask hanging at his chest, a sanitation toolkit on his belt, the jumpsuit has chemical stains that are permanent, the most thankless essential job on any station

sharp-dressed human woman in her 40s who is the closest thing to wealthy you can get on a Compact station, wearing a tailored dark blue civilian suit jacket over a cream blouse, pressed trousers, low heels, a slim data case, a station bar association badge, her clothes are the best quality available but still have that Compact machine-pressed institutional quality — even money cannot buy individual style here

human male musician in his 20s with long hair tied back and calloused fingertips, wearing a soft faded grey thermal shirt and comfortable dark trousers, light shoes, a battered string instrument case slung across his back, a tip jar data chip reader clipped to his belt, he plays in the station common areas for credits and is one of the few sources of non-institutional culture on the station

human woman in her 30s with a tech-focused demeanor, wearing a dark grey technical coverall with an automation department patch, a diagnostic wrist unit with multiple screens, a tool belt sized for small precision instruments not heavy wrenches, anti-static shoes, she manages the maintenance drones and her clothes are clean because she does not do manual labor — she tells machines to do it

stunningly beautiful human woman in a haute couture space-themed evening gown with flowing silver fabric and crystal accents, high heels, elaborate upswept hair, dramatic makeup, designer jewelry, the gown has a thigh-high slit and plunging neckline, she stands on a runway pose, this is fashion not function, haute couture not shipboard life

human male in full Victorian steampunk attire with brass goggles on a top hat, a waistcoat with watch chains, leather spats, gears and cogs decorating every surface, a steam-powered prosthetic arm with visible pistons, copper tubing winding around everything, this is steampunk cosplay not lived-in sci-fi, the aesthetic is retro-futurism not working-class space

two identical human women standing side by side in exactly matching pristine white Compact uniforms, same hairstyle same posture same expression, they look like clones or mannequins not individuals, the uniforms are brand new and identical down to the crease, violates single character rule and too-clean rule simultaneously
